adds.rationale: Pure-play US merchant nuclear utility (largest carbon-free generator in the country) that spun off from Exelon Feb 1, 2022 and now trades cleanly as a single name; complements the URA/CCJ mining exposure and BWXT reactor-services exposure already in the watchlist with the actual operator of the existing US reactor fleet, which is the direct beneficiary of the April 2022 federal civil-nuclear-credit program.

news_evidence.summary: Constellation Energy completes separation from Exelon and begins regular-way trading on Nasdaq under CEG, becoming the largest US producer of carbon-free electricity with a ~22 GW nuclear fleet
Constellation reports Q1 2022 results: nuclear fleet generated ~43 TWh in the quarter; management calls out 'strong financial and operational progress' as standalone
Biden administration launches $6B Civil Nuclear Credit Program to keep at-risk existing US reactors open, a direct subsidy tailwind for the merchant nuclear fleet CEG operates

adds.rationale: Prime contractor for the US Space Development Agency's low-Earth-orbit constellation and the Space Force's deep-space radar, providing single-name exposure to the militarized-space buildout that the user's wave thesis names (rockets/spacecraft) and that complements RKLB (small launch) and ARKX (broad space ETF) with a profitable mega-cap operator; the ITA defense ETF in the current watchlist underweights space specifically.

news_evidence.summary: Space Development Agency selects Northrop Grumman for a $692M Transport Layer Tranche 1 award to build 42 LEO mesh-communication satellites, a foundational piece of the Pentagon's proliferated-LEO architecture
US Space Force awards Northrop Grumman $341M Deep-Space Advanced Radar Capability (DARC) contract for space-domain-awareness sensing

adds.rationale: Machine-vision and barcode-reader maker that supplies the 'eyes' for factory and warehouse automation; provides a profitable, listed-since-1989 single-name robotics holding to pair with the BOTZ industrial-robotics ETF and ISRG surgical-robotics, addressing the user's named 'robotics' next-wave bucket with a company whose Q1 2022 results already show the wave is in commercial buildup (logistics now its largest end market, +18% YoY revenue).

news_evidence.summary: Cognex Q1 2022 revenue $282M, +18% YoY; logistics is now the company's largest end market, with management citing broad recovery in factory automation including automotive

rationale_overall: With 20 tickers already in the watchlist and 4 slots free, the gaps are no longer about coverage breadth but about depth in the user's three named 'next waves' that still trail AI in single-name density: nuclear operations (URA/CCJ are miners, BWXT is reactor services, none operate reactors), proliferated-LEO space (RKLB and ARKX skew small-launch and broad-ETF), and physical robotics (BOTZ and ISRG are an ETF and a surgical-only specialist). CEG, NOC, and CGNX each add one profitable, liquid, US-listed single-name in those three buckets, lifting nuclear to 4 (the per-bucket ceiling), rockets/spacecraft to 3, and robotics to 3. Post-change size is 23 of 24; one slot held in reserve. No removes: every current ticker still maps to a wave the profile names, and at quarter 6 the watchlist is still in buildup phase rather than rotation.
